group.name: Binding policy prose user-declared unfollowable — clarity defect tracked nowhere

statement: CLAUDE.md line 100's Autonomous-loop authorization paragraph — which the user challenged anyone to defend as clear and easy to follow — remains the same single ~200-word run-on at HEAD (commit 82500702 removed only the six iteration-cap words, not the structure), and no substrate item, decision, or gap tracks the clarity defect; both window session-audit runs surfaced it as the still-open DA-01 with no direction given.
action: File the clarity defect as a tracked substrate item so it stops being recorded nowhere, and restructure the CLAUDE.md:100 paragraph only on the user's direction (whether/when/how is his call — the user rebuked the assistant for presuming his decisions); any restructuring must not re-introduce the rejected iteration-cap mechanism or add register-violating narration.

in: a substrate tracking item for the clarity defect
out: the iteration-cap termination-mechanism residue (KB-25 — tasks.json TASK-101 half still open; the CLAUDE.md cap words are already deleted in 82500702)
rewriting the paragraph without user direction

verify: A substrate item/decision explicitly tracks the CLAUDE.md:100 clarity defect, or the paragraph is restructured on user direction into prose no longer a single run-on fusing multiple rules; the defect is no longer tracked nowhere.

group.name: Substrate lifecycle-status bookkeeping drift (stored buckets lag completed reality; standing validator warnings)

statement: FEAT-011 and FEAT-014 remain stored `in-progress` although every member task under them is completed (their wave-2/3 gaps FGAP-125/126/128 read closed at HEAD), leaving 22 task-completed-feature-complete validator warnings standing — the stored feature bucket no longer reflects the completion state the substrate itself derives.
action: Truth-up FEAT-011 and FEAT-014 to their TRUE bucket per the completion-sequence step-7 policy — `complete` only if every acceptance criterion is actually met, `in-review` with named residue otherwise, filing tasks for any genuinely remaining work — clearing the 22 standing warnings honestly, never by faking completion.

in: FEAT-011 and FEAT-014 stored status in .context/features.json
out: re-opening the closed member gaps (FGAP-125/126/128 closures stand as filed)
the re-rendered FEAT-014 residual atoms KB-08/KB-11/KB-12 (their own ground)

verify: context-validate reports zero task-completed-feature-complete warnings for FEAT-011/FEAT-014; each feature's stored status matches its true acceptance-criteria state through the sanctioned write path.

group.name: Substrate lifecycle-status bookkeeping drift (stored buckets lag completed reality; standing validator warnings)

statement: DEC-0024, DEC-0025 and DEC-0026 still read status `open` although the gaps they decide (FGAP-093, FGAP-140, FGAP-145) are closed and their implementing tasks completed — enacted decisions carried in the substrate as undecided.
action: Move DEC-0024/0025/0026 to their true status (enacted) through the sanctioned write path, matching the closed/completed state of the gaps and tasks each one decides.

in: DEC-0024, DEC-0025, DEC-0026 stored status in .context/decisions.json
out: the decided gaps' own closure records (FGAP-093/140/145 stand as filed)

verify: decisions.json shows DEC-0024/0025/0026 off `open` (enacted) with attested writes; no audit/validator surface still lists the three as open decisions over closed subject matter.

group.name: Projection-surface staleness (committed derived views lag the substrate they project)

statement: html-views/substrate-overview.html has not been regenerated since wave-2 closure commit e32b164a although 8 later commits touch .context, against CLAUDE.md's documented build-html-views.ts projection of the active substrate to that view — the committed projection surface misrepresents the current substrate.
action: Re-run `npx tsx scripts/orchestrator/build-html-views.ts` against the current substrate and commit the regenerated view; if regeneration is meant to be bound to substrate changes, record that binding so the surface cannot silently lag again.
verify: html-views/substrate-overview.html's last commit is at or after the newest .context-touching commit and the view reflects the current substrate state (no substrate commit after it without a regeneration).

group.name: De-jargon program residue on shipped catalog surfaces

statement: Planning-cycle vocabulary (Cycle-N phrasing) still ships in the packaged catalog schema descriptions installed into every consuming substrate (the 18 sample schemas' oid descriptions) and in pi-context test-file comments — carriers inside the de-jargon program's filed scope that the program never reached.
action: Extend the de-jargon cleanup to the shipped carriers: rewrite the Cycle-N planning vocabulary out of the packaged sample schema descriptions (dual-surface with any live schema copies, through the sanctioned write/schema path) and out of the pi-context test-file comments, within the program's filed scope.

in: pi-context test-file comments carrying the vocabulary

verify: grep for Cycle-N planning vocabulary over packages/pi-context/samples/schemas/*.json and the affected test files returns no matches; the de-jargon program's carrier set records these surfaces closed.
